task_completion.description: The title is centered, bold, and set to 16pt font size.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the main title ('Regional Climate Variability...') at the top of the document. It should be horizontally centered, in bold text, and visibly larger than the surrounding text.

task_completion.description: The authors line is centered, italicized, and set to 12pt font size.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the authors line ('Hansen, J., Sato, M.,...') directly below the title. It should be horizontally centered, in italics, and visibly smaller than the title.

task_completion.description: Heading 1 style is applied to the main sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the headings: Abstract, Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion, Conclusion, and References. They should be formatted with the 'Heading 1' style (typically larger, bold, and distinct from body text), and the style indicator in the toolbar should show 'Heading 1' when selected.

task_completion.description: Heading 2 style is applied to the subsections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the headings: Data Collection, Statistical Analysis, Temperature Trends, and Precipitation Patterns. They should be formatted with the 'Heading 2' style (distinct from Heading 1 and body text), and the style indicator in the toolbar should show 'Heading 2' when selected.

task_completion.description: Body text paragraphs are set to justified alignment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the standard paragraphs in the document. Both the left and right margins of the text blocks should be straight and flush (justified), rather than ragged on the right side.

task_completion.description: A 0.5-inch hanging indent is applied to all entries in the References section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the References section at the end of the document. The first line of each citation should be flush left, while all subsequent lines of the same citation should be indented to the right by 0.5 inches.

Look for evidence of saving, such as clicking the 'Save' icon (floppy disk), using the File > Save menu, or the absence of an 'unsaved changes' indicator (like an asterisk) in the window title bar at the end of the trajectory.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical word processor (e.g., LibreOffice Writer, Microsoft Word) to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the UI of a word processing application with formatting toolbars, rather than a plain text editor or terminal window.

integrity.description: The agent formatted the actual provided document rather than creating a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the document should match the climate science paper content (e.g., mentions of temperature trends, precipitation, and real citations like Hansen et al. 2010), not just empty headings.

integrity.description: The agent used proper paragraph formatting tools for indents and alignment rather than manually typing spaces.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of the paragraph settings dialog, ruler adjustments, or toolbar buttons for hanging indents and justified text, rather than the agent repeatedly pressing the spacebar or tab key.
